title: CypherX JWT Claims (Contract 1)
description: The CLAIMS object of every JWT issued by SharedCore/Auth. The JWT header (alg=RS256, typ=JWT, kid) is a separate structure and is NOT modelled here. iss and aud are deployment-configurable (resolved from AUTH_ISSUER_URL / AUTH_PLATFORM_AUDIENCE) — this schema describes shape, not literal values. Forward-compatibility (Contract 1 R-forward-compat): verifiers MUST NOT reject tokens that contain unrecognised optional claims, hence additionalProperties is true and only validation-mandatory claims are required.

iss.description: Issuer. Resolves to the deployment's AUTH_ISSUER_URL (e.g. https://auth.cypherx.ai for CypherX cloud, operator-set for self-hosted/white-label). Verifiers MUST match against their configured AUTH_ISSUER_URL or one of the configured trusted issuers in federation mode — never a hardcoded literal.

sub.description: Subject — the agent_id (agent-uuid) the token was minted for.

aud.description: Audience. Array of strings. MUST contain the configured AUTH_PLATFORM_AUDIENCE of the verifying deployment.

iat.description: Issued-at, seconds since the Unix epoch (NumericDate, RFC 7519).

exp.description: Expiry, seconds since the Unix epoch (NumericDate). MUST be in the future (clock-skew tolerance ±60s). Token lifetime (exp - iat) MUST NOT exceed 1 hour (3600s) for agent tokens.

jti.description: Unique token id (UUID v4). Used for replay detection (TOKEN_REPLAYED).

tenant_id.description: Org/isolation-boundary UUID (Contract 13). MUST be present and non-empty.

agent_id.description: Agent UUID. MUST be present and non-empty. Normally equals sub.

agent_version.description: Semantic version of the agent definition (e.g. 1.0.0).

api_key_id.description: UUID of the API key the token was exchanged from (Contract 18). Renamed from key_id to avoid confusion with the JWT header 'kid'. Present when the token originated from an API-key exchange.

deployment_id.description: Disambiguates tokens when a service trusts more than one issuer (e.g. cypherx-prod, acme-selfhosted). Used by regional gateways accepting both auth.cypherx.ai and a customer's self-hosted Auth via federation.

scopes.description: Granted scopes (e.g. llm:invoke, memory:read, memory:write, rag:query, tool:invoke, guardrails:check). The required scope for an action MUST be present.

plan.description: Billing/entitlement plan of the tenant (e.g. free, pro, enterprise).

region.description: Deployment region the token is scoped to (e.g. us-east-1).

cnf.description: RESERVED (⚡ reserved / 📋 enforced Phase 2 Component 3b). Token binding (RFC 7800): { "x5t#S256": "<...>" } for mTLS-bound, { "jkt": "<...>" } for DPoP-bound. May be null when unbound. Verifiers MUST accept its presence now; enforcement (TOKEN_BINDING_MISMATCH) turns on in Phase 2.

x5t#S256.description: Base64url SHA-256 thumbprint of the mTLS client certificate.

jkt.description: JWK SHA-256 thumbprint of the DPoP proof-of-possession key.

wkl_id.description: RESERVED (📋 enforced Phase 2 Component 8c). SPIFFE workload identity URI (e.g. spiffe://cypherx.prod/ns/xagent/sa/agent-runtime). May be null. Defined now for alignment; enforced later.

behavior_policy_id.description: RESERVED (📋 enforced Phase 2 Component 5c). UUID of the behavioral envelope policy (Contract 17) applied to this agent. May be null. Defined now; enforced later.

delegation_chain.description: RESERVED (📋 enforced Phase 2 Component 8d). A2A delegation chain (Contract 3). A2A tokens only. Defined now; enforced later.

delegation_depth.description: RESERVED (📋 Phase 2). A2A delegation chain depth. A2A tokens only.

delegation_root_task.description: RESERVED (📋 Phase 2). Task id at the root of the A2A delegation chain. A2A tokens only.

delegation_root_expiry.description: RESERVED (📋 Phase 2). NumericDate expiry of the root delegation. A2A tokens only.

approval_context.description: RESERVED (📋 enforced Phase 2 / Contract 16). Reference to a step-up approval grant. Present on action-call tokens only when an approval-required scope is invoked. Defined now; enforced later.
